title: Add optional username to Site persistence + sites:setUsername IPC/bridge/types + store action
acceptance_criteria: [ ] The Site entity in app/src-electron/sites.ts gains an optional `username` field (string, defaults empty/null); CREATE TABLE adds a `username` column and existing databases are migrated additively (ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN guarded) so old data still loads
[ ] A `sites:setUsername` IPC handler persists the username for a given site id; list/add continue to return the username on each Site row
[ ] The preload bridge (StarSitesApi) exposes `setUsername(id, username)` and the StarSite type in app/src/env.d.ts carries the new `username` field
[ ] A `setSiteUsername(id, username)` action exists on the Pinia app-store, calls the bridge, and mirrors the value into local state
[ ] Only a username is stored — no password field is added anywhere
[ ] Saved username survives an app restart (persisted to SQLite, not just store state)
notes: Extends the existing BRWSR-002 sites store. Keep migration additive — do not drop/recreate the table. Scope: persistence + wiring only; UI lives in SITEUSR-002/003.

title: Add per-site username input to the Settings Job-sites list, wired to save & persist
acceptance_criteria: [ ] Each row in the Settings Job-sites list (SettingsPage.vue) shows an editable free-text username field alongside the existing host/enabled/remove controls
[ ] Entering a username and saving calls store.setSiteUsername and the saved value is shown next to that site (Scenario 1)
[ ] The field is optional — a site with no username renders an empty/placeholder input and saves nothing harmful
[ ] The saved username re-appears after navigating away and back, and after an app restart (via hydrateSites)
notes: UI-only ticket against SettingsPage.vue. Reuse the existing .site row layout and store actions; no password field.

title: Show saved username + silent copy button alongside the active Discover tab (none when unset)
acceptance_criteria: [ ] When the active Discover tab's site has a saved username, the username is displayed alongside the embedded browser for that tab (Scenario 2)
[ ] A copy button is shown next to the displayed username and copies the username to the clipboard with no confirmation message (silent) (Scenario 2)
[ ] When the active tab's site has no saved username, no username and no copy button are displayed (Scenario 3)
[ ] The displayed username tracks the selected tab — switching tabs updates (or hides) it to match the newly active site
notes: UI-only ticket against DiscoverPage.vue. Display surface is the embedded-browser chrome/tab area for the matching site only. Copy is silent (no toast). Depends only on SITEUSR-001, independent of SITEUSR-002.

title: Evaluate BDD UC scenarios end-to-end
acceptance_criteria: [ ] Walk every acceptance scenario in bdd_SITE_USERNAME.md against the actual implementation
[ ] For each scenario, verify Given/When/Then behaviour matches exactly; flag deviations
[ ] Add an end-to-end test covering at least the happy path (save username in Settings, see it + copy it in Discover) in the project's existing test suite (no new test framework)
[ ] If any scenario fails, raise a new ticket with the failure repro rather than silently fixing in this one
notes: This is the safety net before the BDD UC's Status flips to DONE. Stricter than per-ticket auto-eval — verify against scenarios, not just per-ticket criteria.
